content:
# Scanning Electron Microscopy

**Summary**: A microscopy technique used to observe surface details and morphology at high magnification.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017]] used SEM/EDX to identify pore filling, pore lining clay minerals, and their morphology.

- [[2001-mohammed|Mohammed, 2001]] used SEM to examine the morphology of bridging additives and the resulting filter cake.

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] applied SEM to identify authigenic clay minerals (illite, chlorite) and quartz overgrowths.

- [[1994-shokoya|Shokoya, 1994]] used SEM to study the general morphology changes of steel samples exposed to drilling fluid.

- [[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988]] applied SEM to obtain detailed compositional and textural data on selected rock samples.

- [[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009]] used SEM to characterize the physical structure and purity of produced MWCNTs.

- [[2012-amao|Amao, 2012]] used SEM to identify euendolithic cyanobacteria from Arabian Gulf samples.

- [[2012-geri|Geri, 2012]] used SEM to provide high resolution pictures of samples and determine chemical compositions qualitatively or semi-quantitatively.

- [[2012-jabbar|JABBAR, 2012]] used SEM to characterize the physical changes on calcite and carbonate surfaces after brine treatment.

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] applied SEM to identify marine cementation and micrite recrystallization.

- [[2013-khan|Khan, 2013]] used SEM to characterize the texture, pore size, and grain structure of Indiana Limestone cores.

- [[2013-murtaza|Murtaza, 2013]] used SEM to examine the microstructural evolution of the slurry.

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] applied SEM analysis on 15 samples to get a clearer idea of grain boundaries, microstructures, and elemental analysis via EDS.

- [[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014]] used SEM to characterize the surface morphology and adsorption potential of fly ash materials.

- [[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014]] used SEM to identify the microporosity in the investigated samples.

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] used SEM to analyze the microfacies and mineralogy of the samples.

- [[2014-kasha|Kasha, 2014]] used SEM to characterize the surface morphology of calcite, dolomite, and MEC chips.

- [[2014-elhag|ELHAG, 2014]] used SEM to observe the microstructure and morphology of bentonite particles.

- [[2014-al-ameri|AL-AMERI, 2014]] used SEM to observe changes in the microcrystalline matrix and pore structure after CO2 exposure.

- [[2015-adebayo|Adebayo, 2015]] used SEM to characterize the pore structures of Indiana limestone samples.

- [[2015-oyehan|Oyehan, 2015]] used SEM to characterize the morphology of isolated bacterial strains.

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] used SEM to observe the surface morphology of the resins before and after metal adsorption.

- [[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015]] used SEM for microstructural characterization of the cement samples.

- [[2015-malik|Malik, 2015]] used SEM to identify and confirm the presence of different bacterial shapes in various media.

- [[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015]] used SEM to observe micro-scale features in the oolitic and skeletal grainstones.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] used SEM to analyze grain morphology and mineral textures in carbonate and siliciclastic facies.

- [[2015-arslan|Arslan, 2015]] used SEM to characterize and identify key species' morphological features.

- [[2015-khan-2|Khan, 2015]] used SEM to analyze the microstructure of cement samples containing carbon nanotubes.

- [[2015-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2015]] used SEM to determine the pore geometry of Indiana limestone core samples.

- [[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]] used SEM to investigate detailed sedimentological features in selected lithofacies.

- [[2016-babker|Babker, 2016]] used SEM to investigate the relationship between quartz, illite, and kaolinite in siltstone lithofacies.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] used SEM to identify micro-scale features like globigerina and miliolids in carbonate facies.

- [[2016-malik|Malik, 2016]] used SEM to observe the texture of rocks and identify minute details in the samples.

- [[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016]] used SEM to determine the shape and size of particulate matter across three cities.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] used SEM to characterize the shape and size of AgNP, Fe2O3, and nZVI particles.

- [[2016-salih|Salih, 2016]] applied SEM to identify micro-porosity, grain shape, cement, and matrix in 18 samples.

- [[2016-geri|GERI, 2016]] used SEM to evaluate the composition of solids before and after the removal process.

- [[2016-alhamad|AlHamad, 2016]] used SEM to determine the size distribution of prepared zeolite nanoparticles.

- [[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017]] used SEM to confirm mineral occurrence and show morphology in various lithofacies.

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] applied SEM to characterize the pore types and organic nanoporosity in the Tuwaiq Mountain source rock.

- [[2017-mohamed|MOHAMED, 2017]] used SEM to confirm particle size and agglomeration after ball milling.

- [[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017]] used SEM to investigate the microfacies and mineralogical details of the carbonate samples.

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] used SEM to image selected specimens for detailed morphological analysis.

- [[2017-budiyanto|Budiyanto, 2017]] used SEM to characterize the morphology of isolated bacterial strains.

- [[2017-ahmed|Ahmed, 2017]] used SEM to analyze the microstructure of the cement with fiber.

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] used FESEM to characterize the morphology of RFA, AFA, and PEFA.

- [[2017-baig|Baig, 2017]] used SEM to observe hydration products and confirm a denser microstructure in cement with Nano zeolite.

- [[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017]] used SEM to observe micrite crystal sizes and dolomite textures.

- [[2017-adam-2|Adam, 2017]] used SEM to analyze microfacies and diagenetic features in the Khartam Member.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] applied SEM to identify mineralogy, cement type, and authigenic clay minerals in 30 selected samples.

- [[2017-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2017]] used SEM to compare the surface reaction of GLDA/DI and GLDA/SW.

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] used SEM to characterize detrital and diagenetic components.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] applied SEM to identify micro-scale minerals, cement types, and micropores under 4 µm.

- [[2018-alqahtani|AlQahtani, 2018]] used SEM to evaluate the ilmenite's elemental composition and purity.

- [[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019]] used SEM coupled with EDX to assess the morphological structure and elemental composition of GPDADMAC and GPHM.

- [[2019-alotaibi|Alotaibi, 2019]] used FESEM to analyze the shape and size of PM10 in Jubail and Ras Tanura.

- [[2019-suleiman-3|Suleiman, 2019]] used SEM to track changes on the rock surface at a microscale level after treatment.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] used SEM to identify four different micrite microtextures based on morphology and contact.

- [[2020-islam|Islam, 2020]] used SEM to investigate pore system geometry, clay mineralogy, and diagenetic features.

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] used SEM to characterize pores, mineralogy, and types of cement in the samples.

- [[2021-alnasser|AlNasser, 2021]] used SEM to characterize the morphology of the prepared PG, showing flake structures.

- [[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021]] applied SEM analysis to characterize texture, cementation, and mineral distribution in core samples.

- [[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021]] used SEM imagery to determine changes in the morphology of biochar samples.

- [[2022-khan|Khan, 2022]] used SEM to examine the micro-texture and mineralogy of core samples.

- [[2021-khan|KHAN, 2021]] used SEM to analyze the morphology of clay samples after interaction with ionic liquids.

- [[2022-salih|Salih, 2022]] used SEM to identify microstructural features like micro-porosity, pore distribution, and grain shape.

- [[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022]] used SEM to analyze mudstone chips, identifying platy detrital and lath-shaped authigenic illite in F1.

- [[2022-aljaberi|AlJaberi, 2022]] used SEM to analyze the shape and size of hematite and ilmenite particles.

- [[2023-nasir|Nasir, 2023]] used SEM to create images of major foraminiferal groups.

- [[2022-xiao|Xiao, 2022]] used SEM to provide qualitative evidence of surface modification and inter-granular fluid distribution.

- [[2022-kalam|Kalam, 2022]] applied SEM to analyze the changes in rock surface morphology before and after surfactant adsorption.

- [[2023-basfar|Basfar, 2023]] used SEM to examine the external morphology and texture of weighting materials.

- [[2023-suleiman|Suleiman, 2023]] used SEM to capture the surface morphology of powdered and solid samples at 10, 50, and 100X magnification.

- [[2023-prayudi|Prayudi, 2023]] used SEM to capture high-resolution images of representative foraminiferal specimens.

- [[2023-baig|Baig, 2023]] used SEM to visualize the morphology of calcium carbonate precipitation in sand samples.

- [[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]] used SEM to image and identify new species like Arenoturrispirillina waskowskae.

- [[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024]] used SEM to illustrate important taxa and their significant features.

- [[2023-khalid|Khalid, 2023]] used SEM to evaluate the surface properties of coatings before and after hydrogen exposure.

- [[2023-ahmed-2|AHMED, 2023]] used SEM to analyze the particle shapes of various cement additives.

- [[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023]] used SEM to confirm that FFA particles have a spherical shape while hematite particles are irregular.

- [[2023-desouky|Desouky, 2023]] used SEM to visualize HAP morphologies such as needle-like, rod-like, and plate-like crystals.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] used SEM to characterize microporosity and investigate micro-features like crystal size and shape.

- [[2024-isah|Isah, 2024]] used SEM and FIB-SEM to characterize nano-to-micro-scale pore structure and connectivity before and after fluid exposure.

- [[2024-alvayed|Alvayed, 2024]] used SEM to validate the bimodal distribution of constituents and map surface features.

- [[2024-jalloh|Jalloh, 2024]] used SEM (JSM-5900LV) to support taxonomic analysis of foraminiferal specimens.

- [[2024-jibrin|Jibrin, 2024]] used SEM images of Leman sandstone to characterize multiscale pore structures.

- [[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024]] used SEM to characterize the physical properties of organoclays (OCs).

- [[2025-almousa|Almousa, 2025]] used SEM to investigate the morphology of the CAC.

- [[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992]] applied SEM to characterize the microfacies and porosity of the Arab-D Limestone Member.

- [[2004-ahmed|Ahmed, 2004]] used SEM to examine the nature of scale deposition throughout the core.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] applied SEM to observe microstructures, grain boundaries, and determine elemental composition of the sediment.

- [[1988-darwish-2|Darwish, 1988]] applied SEM-EDS to provide detailed compositional and textural data on 16 selected rock samples.

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] used SEM to observe the cryptocrystalline nature of magnesite and perform chemical scanning.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Used to characterize the micro-morphology of pores and associated minerals. ([[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017, p. 46]])

- Used to visualize the size distribution of calcium carbonate particles (Samples A, B, C, D). ([[2001-mohammed|Mohammed, 2001, p. 16]])

- Confirmed the authigenic origin of illite as fibrous/lath-like structures and chlorite as delicate platelet crystals. ([[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001, p. 72]])

- Identified that the drilling fluid attacks the grain boundaries of the steel. ([[1994-shokoya|Shokoya, 1994, p. 18]])

- Provided detailed texture for oolitic grains and evidence of diagenetic processes like neomorphism in dolomite. ([[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988, p. 41]])

- The SEM images showed that the product was pure and only MWCNTs were observed with a diameter range of 20-40 nm. ([[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009, p. 70]])

- SEM was used to confirm the presence of micro-boring in calcite grains during re-boring tests. ([[2012-amao|Amao, 2012, p. 125]])

- SEM was used to provide the morphology of the filter cake and identify particulate plugging of the invaded zone. ([[2012-geri|Geri, 2012, p. 49]])

- Surface modification was observed as heterogeneous across both calcite and carbonate samples. ([[2012-jabbar|JABBAR, 2012, p. 104]])

- Identified two different scales of resolution for marine calcite cement. ([[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013, p. 90]])

- SEM images revealed that at high resolution (1 µm), pores and grains could be clearly identified in both treated and untreated zones. ([[2013-khan|Khan, 2013, p. 107]])

- SEM showed that the microstructure of hardened slurry changes from a three-dimensional fiber network to a block structure at high temperatures. ([[2013-murtaza|Murtaza, 2013, p. 58]])

- SEM was useful in getting a clear idea of grain boundaries, microstructures, and identifying clay types based on morphology. ([[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014, p. 32]])

- SEM revealed that raw fly ash has an orbicular and heterogeneous pore structure with an average grain diameter of approximately 100 µm. ([[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014, p. 60]])

- Identified microporosity within the dolomite crystals and between sparry calcite crystals. ([[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014, p. 17]])

- Not stated in source ([[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]])

- SEM images revealed distinct mineral features and allowed for the identification of oil deposits on carbonate surfaces. ([[2014-kasha|Kasha, 2014, p. 123]])

- Provided high-resolution images showing particle shapes and textures. ([[2014-elhag|ELHAG, 2014]])

- Microphotographs showed structural changes in Indiana, Pink Desert, and Khuff limestone samples. ([[2014-al-ameri|AL-AMERI, 2014, p. 61]])

- Used to interpret experimental results regarding carbonate mineralogy and pore structure. ([[2015-adebayo|Adebayo, 2015, p. 70]])

- Not stated in source ([[2015-oyehan|Oyehan, 2015, p. 47]])

- SEM was used in conjunction with EDX to analyze the structure of the resin. ([[2015-musa|Musa, 2015, p. 65]])

- Used to evaluate the microstructure of the cement slurry under HPHT conditions. ([[2015-thalab|Thalab, 2015, p. 33]])

- SEM images showed that all the bacteria are rod shaped, but with varying sizes. ([[2015-malik|Malik, 2015, p. 44]])

- Revealed moldic porosity and interparticle porosity in various lithofacies. ([[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015, p. 79]])

- SEM images showed quartz grains at 100 microns coated by clay minerals like palygorskite. ([[2015-ali|Ali, 2015, p. 118]])

- SEM was used to capture detailed imagery for identification against established catalogs. ([[2015-arslan|Arslan, 2015, p. 48]])

- Used to evaluate the physical properties and structural changes in the cement slurry. ([[2015-khan-2|Khan, 2015, p. 31]])

- SEM images showed no clay particles in the specific areas observed. ([[2015-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2015, p. 66]])

- SEM was applied on four selected lithofacies to get more detailed sedimentological and petrophysical properties. ([[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016, p. 45]])

- SEM results showed the growth of quartz and the presence of mudstone minerals such as illite and kaolinite. ([[2016-babker|Babker, 2016, p. 73]])

- SEM images confirmed the presence of planktonic foraminifera (Globigerina) and benthic Miliolids in various grainstones. ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016, p. 82]])

- SEM images revealed detailed mineral textures, such as calcite crystals in a fine matrix (Fig 4.3) or micritized fabrics (Fig 4.12). ([[2016-malik|Malik, 2016, p. 10]])

- SEM images showed particles as small as 1 µm with various shapes including angular, flaky, and rod-like. ([[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016, p. 88]])

- All images showed nanoclusters and agglomerates of spherically shaped nanoparticles with sizes ranging between 30 and 50 nm. ([[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016, p. 88]])

- Identified micro-porosity and small scale diagenetic features in various lithofacies. ([[2016-salih|Salih, 2016, p. 46]])

- SEM was used to characterize the morphology of barite and filter cake solids. ([[2016-geri|GERI, 2016, p. 48]])

- Particles were found in the 50-70 nm range after 35 minutes of centrifugation, suitable for avoiding pore plugging. ([[2016-alhamad|AlHamad, 2016, p. 51]])

- SEM was used to confirm the presence of kaolinite and muscovite within the siltstone and shale units. ([[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017, p. 63]])

- SEM revealed a moderately to well-connected organic pore system with 84% of pores being less than 1µm. ([[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017, p. 53]])

- Confirmed that ball milling for a long time caused small particles to agglomerate. ([[2017-mohamed|MOHAMED, 2017, p. 79]])

- Used to observe skeletal fragments, ooids, and other minerals in various lithofacies (e.g., D1, D2). ([[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017, p. 53]])

- SEM was used to identify specific features like the terminal slit-like aperture in Pseudonubeculina arabica. ([[2017-amao|Amao, 2017, p. 20]])

- Morphological characterization was performed using Scanning Electron Microscope JEOL at 10 keV. ([[2017-budiyanto|Budiyanto, 2017, p. 47]])

- Used for analyzing the microstructural characteristics of the cement slurry. ([[2017-ahmed|Ahmed, 2017, p. 17]])

- RFA showed a hollow shape with heterogenous pore structures (2-50 nm) and average diameter of 100 microns. ([[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017, p. 58]])

- Hydration products were modified with the addition of Nano zeolite as indicated by SEM. ([[2017-baig|Baig, 2017, p. 20]])

- Under SEM, the micrite crystals are observed to be less than 10 µm and typically around 2-5 µm. ([[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017, p. 124]])

- Used to identify lithofacies at microscopic scales. ([[2017-adam-2|Adam, 2017, p. 51]])

- Identified moldic and intraparticle porosity as dominant pore types in the upper section. ([[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017, p. 100]])

- Showed that GLDA/SW produced a more uniform reaction on the rock surface than GLDA/DI. ([[2017-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2017, p. 102]])

- SEM was used to identify diagenetic clays (less than 1% by volume) and other mineral phases. ([[2018-butt|Butt, 2018, p. 24]])

- SEM provided evidence of the dissolution of ooid grains in certain facies. ([[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018, p. 83]])

- Confirmed ilmenite was clean without any contamination. ([[2018-alqahtani|AlQahtani, 2018, p. 45]])

- Characterization results showed that graphene was successfully functionalized for both adsorbents. ([[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019, p. 15]])

- Jubail samples showed smaller, irregular particles (1.4-2.8 $\mu$m) compared to larger, more spherical particles in Ras Tanura (5-7 $\mu$m). ([[2019-alotaibi|Alotaibi, 2019, p. 154]])

- SEM images showed that grains in Khuff samples appeared wrapped by a plastic film after microemulsion treatment. ([[2019-suleiman-3|Suleiman, 2019, p. 65]])

- SEM photomicrographs showed four different micrite microtextures that influence reservoir quality in terms of porosity, permeability, and resistivity. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 20]])

- SEM images showed that kaolinite was a primary filler of pores in the sandstone. ([[2020-islam|Islam, 2020, p. 19]])

- Identified iron oxide between grains in the Dhurma Formation. ([[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021, p. 76]])

- The SEM images confirmed the formation of polymeric chains on the surface of the graphene sheets. ([[2021-alnasser|AlNasser, 2021, p. 48]])

- Used to identify fine-scale features like pyrite framboids and the specific nature of carbonate cements. ([[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021, p. 45]])

- SEM was used to identify morphological changes in biochar produced at 500°C and 800°C. ([[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021, p. 12]])

- SEM provided high-resolution images of oolitic grains and skeletal fragments in the carbonate units. ([[2022-khan|Khan, 2022, p. 47]])

- SEM analysis will be used to support the presence of clay minerals and classification of clay, showing pore structure and embedded minerals. ([[2021-khan|KHAN, 2021, p. 50]])

- SEM was used to detect very fine dolomite crystals (less than 10 microns) in lithofacies LF6. ([[2022-salih|Salih, 2022, p. 58]])

- SEM analysis showed platy detrital and elongated lath-shaped authigenic illite in F1. ([[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022, p. 56]])

- Showed that hematite particles have an irregular shape with sharp boundaries. ([[2022-aljaberi|AlJaberi, 2022, p. 55]])

- Used to visualize and identify foraminiferal morphology in both core samples. ([[2023-nasir|Nasir, 2023, p. 61]])

- SEM can be used as a qualitative method that detects wettibility change and helps to understand the mechanisms. ([[2022-xiao|Xiao, 2022, p. 28]])

- SEM showed that sandstone surfaces became rougher and formed flocs after GS12 adsorption, while carbonate surfaces remained largely unchanged. ([[2022-kalam|Kalam, 2022, p. 70]])

- Used to determine chemical composition (via EDS) and crystalline structure. ([[2023-basfar|Basfar, 2023, p. 46]])

- SEM was used to visualize the morphology of asphaltene molecules and their interaction with mineral surfaces. ([[2023-suleiman|Suleiman, 2023, p. 43]])

- Used to identify and characterize morphological deformities in both SBF and LBF. ([[2023-prayudi|Prayudi, 2023, p. 64]])

- SEM showed that dolomite crystals coexisted with calcite when both MgCl2 and CaCl2 were used (solutions 2, 3, and 4). ([[2023-baig|Baig, 2023, p. 41]])

- The NeoScope JCM-7000 SEM allowed for the resolution of morphological features of newly identified species. ([[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024, p. 50]])

- Used to identify 59 foraminiferal taxa, including several new species. ([[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024, p. 33]])

- Used to assess physical degradation in the coating material after 30 days of aging. ([[2023-khalid|Khalid, 2023, p. 71]])

- Analyzed the physical structure and shape of barite, hematite, ilmenite, Micromax, laponite, perlite, and vermiculite. ([[2023-ahmed-2|AHMED, 2023, p. 92]])

- SEM images confirmed the distinct morphologies of FFA (spherical) and hematite (irregular). ([[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023, p. 53]])

- SEM images revealed various HAP morphologies including needle-like, rod-like, plate-like, and rose-like shapes. ([[2023-desouky|Desouky, 2023, p. 41]])

- SEM images were used to determine that intercrystalline microporosity ranged from 3-18%. ([[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024, p. 14]])

- FIB-SEM allows for high 3D spatial resolution, revealing nanometric pore structures that are otherwise difficult to see. ([[2024-isah|Isah, 2024, p. 40]])

- Confirmed the presence of organic material, clay, and minerals like pyrite and silicate in the sample. ([[2024-alvayed|Alvayed, 2024, p. 23]])

- SEM was used to confirm the identification of 33 species across 19 genera. ([[2024-jalloh|Jalloh, 2024, p. 37]])

- SEM images provided a resolution of 0.174 x 0.174 μm per pixel, allowing for the identification of five distinct rock types. ([[2024-jibrin|Jibrin, 2024, p. 41]])

- SEM was used to observe that surfactants coat the surface of SEP, leading to a decline in surface area. ([[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024, p. 38]])

- CAC has a heterogenous morphology with an amorphous porous surface that looks like a sponge. ([[2025-almousa|Almousa, 2025, p. 40]])

- SEM is helpful in the characterization of rocks due to high resolution, large depth of field, and direct viewing of samples. ([[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992, p. 48]])

- SEM analysis confirmed the presence of mineral deposits at both inlet and outlet faces. ([[2004-ahmed|Ahmed, 2004, p. 62]])

- The observed textural morphologies from SEM study showed that the associated claystone is mainly composed of authigenic kaolinite. ([[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999, p. 18]])

- Identified pyrite crystals in a dolomite matrix and confirmed the authigenic nature of palygorskite fibers. ([[1988-darwish-2|Darwish, 1988, p. 70]])

- SEM revealed that magnesite comprises compact clusters of anhedral crystals. ([[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985, p. 70]])
